content.directive: Should mindX adopt OpenRouter as the primary cloud LLM backplane for the autonomous improvement loop?
content.reasoning: Adopting OpenRouter as PRIMARY backplane creates unacceptable architectural risk for the autonomous improvement loop. Key concerns: (1) Single point of failure - autonomous operations cannot tolerate external service downtime; (2) Vendor lock-in violates cypherpunk principle of owning the stack; (3) No sustainability plan for fallback when external service fails; (4) Technical debt risk from external dependency without migration path. The autonomous loop requires infrastructure sovereignty - primary inference must remain under mindX control with cloud as supplementary fallback. Recommend: keep local/vLLM/Ollama as primary with OpenRouter as optional supplementary provider, not primary backplane.
